Functions for managing worker event loops and launching foreground apps.

# Purpose
The file `worker.h` is a C header file that declares functions related to worker processes within a software application. It provides the function [`worker_event_loop`](<#worker_event_loop>), which manages the event loop for worker processes and blocks execution until the worker is ready to exit. Additionally, it declares the function [`worker_launch_app`](<#worker_launch_app>), which is responsible for launching the foreground application associated with the worker. This header file is part of a larger module, as indicated by the group annotations for documentation purposes.
# Function Declarations (Public API)

---
### worker\_event\_loop<!-- {{#callable_declaration:worker_event_loop}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/applib/worker.h#L28>)

Runs the event loop for a worker until it is ready to exit.
- **Description**: Use this function in the main function of a worker to start and maintain an event loop. It continuously processes events until a termination event occurs, at which point it exits the loop and returns control to the main function. This function must be called in a context where it can block indefinitely, as it will not return until the worker is ready to exit.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`worker_event_loop`](<worker.c.md#worker_event_loop>)  (Implementation)


---
### worker\_launch\_app<!-- {{#callable_declaration:worker_launch_app}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/applib/worker.h#L31>)

Launches the foreground app for the worker.
- **Description**: Use this function to start the foreground application associated with a worker. It is typically called within the worker's execution context to initiate the application that the worker is responsible for managing. Ensure that the worker environment is correctly set up before calling this function to avoid unexpected behavior.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`worker_launch_app`](<worker.c.md#worker_launch_app>)  (Implementation)
